mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of equity cash flows and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns as earnings forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances when UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp, or the signal loses predictive power during economic expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and generates positive market returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when both series grow, or the interaction term shows negative IC in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side bottlenecks, leading to negative equity returns as profit margins compress.
failure_condition: Equity returns do not turn negative when INDPRO drops below its trend, or the signal predicts positive returns during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly gains in total nonfarm payrolls above 200,000 indicate robust economic activity and wage growth, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.01 over rolling windows, or 5-day forward returns are uncorrelated with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises above 3.0% year-over-year, inflation expectations accelerate and force monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in over 60% of cases when CPI exceeds the 3% threshold, or the signal loses significance during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP and falling retail sales signals weakening consumer demand despite aggregate growth, causing earnings revisions to turn negative and driving down equity prices over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or both series moving in opposite directions shows no predictive IC over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid expansion in industrial production combined with stable inflation indicates healthy capacity utilization and margin expansion, generating positive market returns over the next month as corporate profits improve.
failure_condition: Forward returns are not significantly positive when both conditions are met, or the interaction effect reverses direction during early-cycle phases.
